# cache/audit

Snapshots produced by repository audits.  The directory is **append-only** — old
manifests are archived alongside new ones so the historical view of what the
tree looked like on a given date stays reproducible.

| File | Date | Notes |
|------|------|-------|
| `2026-03-20-command-log.txt`     | 2026-03-20 | Commands executed during the March audit. Stale relative to current `master`. |
| `2026-03-20-file-manifest.txt`   | 2026-03-20 | Tree snapshot; **stale** — references removed Python/PyO3 binding crate, missing `forex-data/src/core/all_indicators.rs`, `hpc_ta.rs`, `parquet_migration.rs`, `quant_features.rs`, `regime_detection.rs`, `session_features.rs`, `vortex_io.rs`. Do **not** use for deletion decisions. |
| `2026-03-20-findings.jsonl`      | 2026-03-20 | Findings emitted by the March audit. |
| `2026-05-03-file-manifest.txt`   | 2026-05-03 | Fresh `git ls-files` snapshot of `master` after the CPU/GPU search fixes and the Python excision merged. Use this one. |

Generated with:

```bash
git ls-files | sort > cache/audit/$(date +%Y-%m-%d)-file-manifest.txt
```

Refresh whenever the workspace adds or removes a tracked file at the
crate-graph level (workspace member added/removed, vendor patch added,
`docs/` reorganised, etc.). The findings file should be regenerated by the
audit tool itself, not by hand.
